expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md the case focus is explicitly stated and demonstrated: "Case focus: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios" and a section titled "Canonical top-layer grouping proposal... (without collapsing scenarios)" plus an explicit "Scenario granularity preservation (anti-compression demonstration)" with concrete examples (e.g., "Cancel button stops execution vs X button cancels running execution are separate scenarios").

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: result.md frames itself as "Phase 4b" and focuses on Phase 4b contract requirements: grouping into "canonical top-layer categories" using the listed taxonomy, preserving granularity, and explicitly states "Do not perform Phase 6-style few-shot cleanup in Phase 4b." No Phase 6 cleanup or other phase-specific artifacts are attempted beyond the Phase 4b grouping behavior.
